(Scandinavian, Celtic, Gaelic, Anglo-Saxon, from the Old Norse name Kjall, meaning 'slender' or 'narrow'). However, it is also considered to have possible Hebrew origins, derived from the Hebrew name 'Kel', meaning 'bald'. In Christian tradition, Kyle is considered a variant of the Greek name 'Cycles', meaning 'sun' or 'circle'.

(Germanic name derived from the Old French name Carolus, itself derived from the Latin name Carolus, which came from the Latin word carus meaning 'dear, beloved, dear one' or from the Old Norse name Karl, which means 'man, free man, ruler, and son of man'. The name is also associated with the Latin name Carolus, which is derived from the Latin word karola or carus, meaning 'joyous, glad, or rejoicing', and from the Latin word carus, meaning 'dear, beloved, or expensive'.)
